articles.title: Weather Update: Thunderstorm and rain alert issued in Delhi, UP, Bihar; know the weather forecast across t ... - informalnewz
articles.full_text:
Today’s Weather Update: Temperatures are on the rise across several states, including Delhi-NCR. The Meteorological Department has issued a heatwave alert stretching from UP to Bihar. Meanwhile, the weather in the hilly regions is set to undergo a change once again. Let’s take a look at the weather forecast for the entire country today.
Weather Update: Several states across the country, including Delhi-NCR, are currently gripped by a heatwave; however, amidst this, the Meteorological Department has issued a warning for thunderstorms and rain across 13 states. The rainfall accompanying these storms may lead to a slight dip in temperatures. According to the IMD, this spell of thunderstorms and rain is expected to persist until April 29. Let’s take a look at the weather forecast for the country today, April 24.
Impact of the Heatwave in Delhi
The heat in Delhi continues to intensify. The situation has reached a point where even the breeze from electric fans now feels hot. According to the Meteorological Department, the maximum temperature during the day is expected to soar up to 43 degrees today. Similar weather conditions are likely to prevail on April 25 as well. However, the weather in the capital is set to take a turn after April 26, with light rainfall expected on April 27 and 28.
Heatwave Alert in UP Today
It is not just Delhi; the weather remains hot in UP today as well. The Meteorological Department has issued a warning regarding heatwave conditions from April 24 to 27, while forecasting a possibility of light rainfall on April 28 and 29. According to the department, hot winds may blow during the afternoon today in Lucknow, Varanasi, Gorakhpur, Prayagraj, and Meerut.
Weather to Change in Bihar Starting Tomorrow
While the heat may be oppressive in Bihar today, a change in weather is expected starting tomorrow—April 25—with several districts likely to witness rainfall accompanied by thunder and lightning. The Meteorological Department has issued a storm alert for tomorrow for districts such as Patna, Gaya, Bhagalpur, and Muzaffarpur.
What will be the temperature in your city today?
The heat wave continues to intensify in Punjab and Chandigarh. For the first time this season, the temperature reached 42.9 degrees Celsius. According to the Meteorological Department, a yellow alert has been issued for heatwave conditions on April 24 and 25. According to the Meteorological Department, a weak western disturbance will be active in the mountains. The weather is expected to change from April 25, which may lead to dust storms and light rain. Thunderstorms and lightning are expected between April 26 and 28, which may provide some relief to the people from the heat.
Heatwave warning in Rajasthan too
The intense heat wave continues in Rajasthan, and the Meteorological Department has issued a heat wave warning in many areas from Thursday. According to this, the weather in the state is expected to remain dry for the next three to four days, and the temperature is expected to rise by two degrees Celsius. During this period, maximum temperatures are forecast to be recorded between 41 and 43 degrees Celsius in most parts, while a heat wave warning has been issued in some parts of northern Rajasthan on April 23-25.
